failedAssertions.reason: Topic selection shows 6 topic categories instead of 10 lectures from course-map.json. The 6 topics are: Advanced Topics, Assembly Basics, Control Flow, Data Representation, I/O Operations, Procedures. This appears to be intentional UI grouping rather than individual lecture selection.

BLOCKED: Application bug - certain question types (code-tracing/output-prediction) do not render answer options in the DOM. Questions like 'SCASB', 'XCHG', 'CALL/RET' show question text but no answer option buttons. This prevents completing diagnostic flow to verify mistake pattern detection.
BLOCKED: Cannot test View Report button without completing full diagnostic session - blocked by missing answer options for certain question types.
BLOCKED: Cannot verify IndexedDB persistence without completing diagnostic - blocked by missing answer options for certain question types.

previousRound.summary: 24/29 passed. P3-DIAG-001 (6 topics vs 10 lectures), P3-DIAG-003/004/006 (browser automation blocked).

systemicIssues.description: P3-DIAG-001: Diagnostic topic selection shows 6 broad topic categories instead of 10 individual lectures. This may be intentional UI grouping but doesn't match the assertion spec.
systemicIssues.rootCause: DiagnosticMode.tsx topic selection UI groups lectures into 6 conceptual categories rather than showing 10 individual lectures
systemicIssues.suggestedFix: If 10 individual lectures should be selectable in diagnostic, update DiagnosticMode topic selection UI to show lectures instead of categories. If grouping is intentional design, update validation contract.

systemicIssues.description: P3-DIAG-003, P3-DIAG-004, P3-DIAG-006: Application bug - certain question types (code-tracing/output-prediction) don't render answer options in DOM. Questions get stuck because user cannot select an answer when options aren't visible.
systemicIssues.rootCause: In DiagnosticMode.tsx, code-tracing and output-prediction question types do not properly render their answer options as clickable buttons. The question text appears but options are missing from the DOM.
systemicIssues.suggestedFix: Investigate why answer options aren't rendering for code-tracing/output-prediction questions. Check the component that renders answer choices - options may exist in state but not be rendered due to a conditional or mapping issue.

round3Findings.evidence: agent-browser CLI commands successfully navigate, click, fill forms, and take snapshots. Multiple diagnostic questions answered successfully (JL/JG/JE/JB, True/False, etc.). Browser automation is NOT the issue - the application has a bug.
questionsAnsweredSuccessfully: A JL, B JG, C JE, D JB - conditional jumps
A Register BX, B Memory at address, C Immediate value, D Indirect addressing
A Both are base registers, B BX is base SI is index, C SI is base BX is index, D Both are index registers
A Loads value at memory, B Loads offset address, C Adds to SI, D Loads as signed
questionsWithMissingOptions: SCASB - If AL = 41h and ES:DI points to 'PROGRAM', what will ZF be after SCASB?
XCHG - If AX = 1234h and BX = 5678h, what are values after XCHG AX, BX?
CALL/RET - What values are pushed to stack when CALL MyProc executes?
round3Findings.conclusion: Browser automation works correctly. The issue is that certain question types (SCASB, XCHG, CALL/RET) are code-tracing or output-prediction questions whose answer options are not being rendered in the DOM.

summary: Round 3 validation confirms 24/29 assertions pass. Browser automation is working correctly - the issue blocking P3-DIAG-003/004/006 is an application bug where certain question types don't render answer options. P3-DIAG-001 remains a design decision (6 topics vs 10 lectures). A fix feature is needed to resolve the missing answer options bug.
